title: Data Access & ORM
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Data Access & ORM.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for JPA specification vs Hibernate implementation, First-level vs second-level caching traps.

content_sections.title: Data Access & ORM
content_sections.content:
### Spring Dependency Injection & Transactional Loops
Spring Boot manages object lifecycles and transactions through IoC container injection.

#### IoC Lifecycle & Bean Scopes
Spring manages beans under defined scopes (Singleton, Prototype, Request, Session). The container initializes and resolves dependencies. To handle circular dependencies (e.g. Bean A depends on B, B on A), Spring uses a **3-Level Cache**:
1.  `singletonObjects`: Fully initialized singleton beans.
2.  `earlySingletonObjects`: Partially initialized beans (instantiated but no fields injected yet).
3.  `singletonFactories`: Bean creation factories that generate early references (including proxies).

#### AOP Self-Invocation Trap
Spring Transactions (`@Transactional`) use proxy wrappers. When a client invokes a method, the proxy intercepts the call to manage database connections and commit/rollback boundaries. *Trap:* If a method inside Bean A calls another `@Transactional` method inside the same Bean A using self-invocation (e.g. `this.helperMethod()`), the call bypasses the proxy, executing without transaction context.

#### JPA N+1 Select Bottleneck
Occurs when fetching parent entities with lazy-loaded children. Spring runs 1 query to fetch $N$ parents, then executes $N$ additional queries to fetch children of each parent. *Mitigation:* Use `JOIN FETCH` queries or Entity Graphs to load parent and child rows in a single DB round-trip.

### Depth Analysis: Data Access & ORM
In production configurations, we analyze how JPA specification vs Hibernate implementation, First-level vs second-level caching traps affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Data Access & ORM
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Data Access & ORM is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Data Access & ORM
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Data Access & ORM loops.
